Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SoftSwitchStatusPanel

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

User control that displays Apple IIe soft switch status information in real-time.

public class SoftSwitchStatusPanel : UserControl, INotifyPropertyChanged, IDataContextProvider, ILogical, IThemeVariantHost, IResourceHost, IResourceNode, IStyleHost, ISetLogicalParent, ISetInheritanceParent, ISupportInitialize, IStyleable, INamed, IInputElement, IDataTemplateHost, ISetterValue

object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a visual panel showing the current state of all Apple IIe soft switches, including memory mapping configuration, video modes, pushbuttons, and annunciators. This is invaluable for debugging, education, and understanding what the emulated Apple IIe is doing.

Display Categories:

  • Memory Mapping: 80STORE, RAMRD, RAMWRT, INTCXROM, ALTZP, SLOTC3ROM
  • Video Modes: PAGE2, HIRES, MIXED, TEXT, 80COL, ALTCHAR, FLASHON
  • Pushbuttons: PB0, PB1, PB2 (game controller buttons)
  • Annunciators: AN0, AN1, AN2, AN3/DGR
  • Language Card: BANK1, PREWRITE, HIGHREAD, HIGHWRITE

Two-Phase Initialization: Similar to MainWindow, this control uses a two-phase initialization pattern:

  1. Constructor: Parameterless (Avalonia requirement), loads XAML
  2. Initialize(): Called by parent window, sets DataContext to view model

Data Binding: All status displays are bound to properties in SystemStatusViewModel, which receives updates from the emulator core via ISystemStatusProvider.

Update Frequency: Updates occur whenever soft switch states change in the emulator. Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel (which samples at 50ms), these updates are event-driven and happen immediately when switches change.

Visibility Control: The panel can be shown/hidden via MainWindow.ShowSoftSwitchStatus property, allowing users to toggle it on/off (Alt+W shortcut).

Constructors

SoftSwitchStatusPanel()

Initializes a new instance of the SoftSwitchStatusPanel class.

public SoftSwitchStatusPanel()

Remarks

XAML Requirement: This parameterless constructor is required by Avalonia's XAML loader. The DataContext is not set here; it must be set via Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel)

Initialization: Only loads the XAML markup. The actual view model binding is deferred to the Initialize() method which is called by the parent MainWindow.

Call Sequence: This constructor is called first (by XAML loader or parent), immediately followed by Initialize() (called by MainWindow.Initialize).

Methods

Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel)

Initializes the panel with its view model and establishes data binding. Must be called by parent window after construction.

public void Initialize(SystemStatusViewModel viewModel)

Parameters

viewModel SystemStatusViewModel

The SystemStatusViewModel containing reactive properties for all 24 soft switch states. Must not be null.

Remarks

Two-Phase Pattern: This method completes the initialization started by the parameterless constructor, setting the DataContext to enable XAML data binding.

Data Binding Setup: Once DataContext is set to the SystemStatusViewModel, all XAML bindings become active and the panel starts displaying real-time soft switch states.

Single Initialization: The _isInitialized guard prevents multiple Initialize() calls, which would be an error since DataContext should only be set once.

Called By: MainWindow.Initialize() during the window's two-phase initialization process. This happens automatically after window construction.

View Model Properties: The SystemStatusViewModel contains 24 reactive boolean properties representing all Apple IIe soft switches. When any switch changes in the emulator, the view model property updates, triggering UI refresh via data binding.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if Initialize is called more than once.

InitializeComponent(bool)

Wires up the controls and optionally loads XAML markup and attaches dev tools (if Avalonia.Diagnostics package is referenced).

[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]
public void InitializeComponent(bool loadXaml = true)

Parameters

loadXaml bool

Should the XAML be loaded into the component.
